name: The Ascent to Pico Mofrechu from Igena — round trip
name_original: La Subida Pico Mofrechu desde Igena — ida y vuelta
region_intro: Hiking around Cangas De Onís offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Picos de Europa National Park, characterized by towering peaks, deep gorges, and lush river valleys. The region features prominent glacial lakes such as Lake Enol and Lake Ercina, alongside dramatic river formations like the Cares Gorge and the Dobra River. This area provides a varied terrain for outdoor activities, ranging from moderate river walks to challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Cangas De Onís The most popular hiking route is The lakes of Covadonga and the Mirador de Rey — circular in the Parque Nacional Picos de Europa, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the iconic glacial lakes and offers panoramic viewpoints within the national park. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Olla de San Vicente and the Puente Medieval on the Río Dobra — Cangas de Onís, a moderate 3.3 miles (5.3 km) path. This trail follows the Dobra River, leading to a natural pool and a historic medieval bridge. Local hikers also love the Vega de Ario Trail, an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain in the Picos de Europa, often completed in about 5 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Cangas De Onís is defined by the rugged peaks of the Picos de Europa, serene glacial lakes, and deep river gorges. The extensive network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside walks to demanding mountain ascents. The routes in Cangas De Onís are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1000 reviews. More than 7000 hikers have used komoot to explore Cangas De Onís's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cangas de Onís?
A: Cangas de Onís offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 100 distinct routes available. These range from easy riverside strolls to challenging mountain ascents within the Picos de Europa National Park.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly hiking routes in Cangas de Onís?
A: Yes, Cangas de Onís has numerous easy and beginner-friendly options. There are over 30 routes classified as easy, perfect for those new to hiking or looking for a relaxed walk. Many of these explore the lush river valleys and lower elevations of the region.

Q: What are some good family-friendly hikes near Cangas de Onís?
A: For families, routes that are generally shorter and have less elevation gain are ideal. The area around the Lakes of Covadonga offers gentle paths, and the walk to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1184798929/la-olla-de-san-vicente-and-the-puente-medieval-on-the-rio-dobra-cangas-de-onis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Olla de San Vicente and the Puente Medieval on the Río Dobra&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.3-mile path that follows the river to a natural pool, which can be a fun destination for children.

Q: Are there any circular walks in the Cangas de Onís area?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A notable example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1217655641/the-lakes-of-covadonga-and-the-mirador-de-rey-circular-in-the-parque-nacional-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The lakes of Covadonga and the Mirador de Rey&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.6-mile circular trail that explores the iconic glacial lakes and offers panoramic viewpoints.

Q: Can I bring my dog on hikes around Cangas de Onís?
A: Many trails in the Cangas de Onís area, especially outside the most sensitive zones of the Picos de Europa National Park, are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Check specific trail regulations for any restrictions within the national park boundaries.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/971417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cares Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, visit the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4350793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago Enol&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/195967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Ercina&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the deep green waters of La Olla de San Vicente on the Dobra River. Viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/190299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrelagos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offer breathtaking panoramas of the glacial lakes.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Cangas de Onís and the Picos de Europa?
A: The best time to hike is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is milder, and trails are more accessible. Summer offers the warmest temperatures, while spring and autumn provide beautiful scenery with fewer crowds. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear and experience due to snow and ice at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any wild swimming spots accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region offers natural pools for wild swimming. The most notable is La Olla de San Vicente on the Dobra River, a large natural pool with beautifully green water, which can be reached via a moderate hike. Always exercise caution and check local conditions before swimming.

Q: How long do hikes near Cangas de Onís typically take?
A: Hike durations vary significantly based on difficulty and distance. Easy routes might take 1-2 hours, while moderate trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1217655641/the-lakes-of-covadonga-and-the-mirador-de-rey-circular-in-the-parque-nacional-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The lakes of Covadonga and the Mirador de Rey&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 3 hours. More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1194011758/vega-de-ario-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega de Ario Trail&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trails in the Picos de Europa from Cangas de Onís?
A: Yes, public transport options are available, especially for popular areas like the Lakes of Covadonga. During peak seasons, there are often bus services from Cangas de Onís to key trailheads within the Picos de Europa National Park. It's advisable to check schedules in advance, as services can be seasonal.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cangas de Onís?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1000 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the towering peaks of Picos de Europa to the serene glacial lakes and dramatic river gorges. The well-maintained trails and breathtaking panoramic views are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any hidden gem hikes or less crowded trails in the area?
A: While popular spots like the Lakes of Covadonga can be busy, exploring trails along the Dobra River or venturing into less-trafficked sections of the Picos de Europa can reveal quieter paths. Look for routes that diverge from the main tourist hubs to discover more secluded natural beauty and authentic experiences.
tips: The beginning of the trail to the high Mofrechu peak is the small town of Igena (Ixena) in Asturias. The town is on the banks of the Zardón River. If you stay in the area, there are some good places to fish on the river.

The 4.2 km trail reaches Mofrechu Peak at an altitude of 872 meters above sea level. After ascending 425 m, there is a panoramic view of the bay of Ribadesella. The trail is designated as medium grade, but could be difficult after rains. Don't forget to bring warm clothes with you. The weather at the summit can be cold and windy.
